issue: Front matter absent from previous complete bundle.
evidence: Previous bundle began with Chapter 1; front matter pages v-ix include contents, abbreviations, and Ringe’s transcription note.

issue: Printed p. 304 was missing from Chapter 4 Markdown.

issue: A prose sentence had multiple words joined without spaces.
Two private-use characters remained in the index for Runic Norse -AR.
evidence: U+F761 U+F772 in index line for “a-stem masc. nom. pl., Runic Norse”.

issue: Residual line-break hyphenation was present after previous passes.
evidence: Chapter 2 had 21 candidates, Chapter 4 had 48, index had 4 in the pre-QA audit.
action: Applied exact cleanup for obvious prose artifacts. Residual candidates are mostly morpheme/stem breaks or code-block boundary issues.

issue: Some prose remains inside fenced text blocks because large paradigms/tables were extracted conservatively.
evidence: Several Chapter 4 prose regions occur while a fenced block is open.
action: Acceptable for search/corpus use; improve only if a polished human-reading edition is needed.

evidence: Index Markdown has structured sections and page refs but no p. 348-406 anchors.
action: Structured CSV/TSV is preferable for navigation; page-anchoring the index can be a later enhancement.

issue: Dense paradigms and technical notation remain corpus-grade, not citation-grade.
evidence: Large paradigm tables and mixed PIE/PGmc notation were the main extraction-risk areas throughout the project.
action: Verify individual forms against the PDF before formal citation or before copying into a lexicon/paradigm generator.
